slides.title: 2. YANG-Push to Message Broker Integration Status Update and Hackathon
3. YANG Message Keys for Message Broker Integration
4. YANG Data Model for Network Incident Management
5. An Architecture for a Network Anomaly Detection Framework
6. SIMAP Modelling based on RFC8345 with some extensions
7. Applicability of RFC8795 YANG data model to  SIMAP
9. Network Management Research Group (NMRG) and Network Management Operations (NMOP) Working Group - Collaboration Opportunities
10. BMP YANG Model for Network Telemetry Messages
11. Model for distributed authorization policy sharing

polls.text: Does the WG agree to go with Option 4 (both approaches)?

chat.author: Benoît Claise
chat.text: Lucia, can you please share the meeting minutes location here

chat.author: Dan Voyer
chat.text: it feels like nmrg is not going fast enough in being ahead of nmop, hence why the idea of merge

chat.author: Alex Huang Feng
chat.text: I agree with Rob, the difference is about the timeframe

chat.author: Benoît Claise
chat.text: timeframe ... and focus  I would say. By "focus",  I mean for example hackathon commitments

chat.author: Thomas Graf
chat.text: @Dan, as Jeferson mentioned, IRTF does multiple explorations and does not have to have consensus on which exploration is the best approach. I suggest that NMOP and NMRG is collaborating on this aspect. Lets try to close the timeline gap in order to bring explorations and experiments more closer together.

chat.author: Alex Huang Feng
chat.text: @Benoit, I feel the only difference is really the problems we are trying to tackle. Both problems (short-term as NMOP and long-term as NMRG) can use the hackthon to explore PoC

chat.author: Thomas Graf
chat.text: @Mahesh, the bmp telemetry message was advertised on IDR, GROW and NMOP and presented at GROW and NMOP. In the previous presentations we asked exactly this question, where this document should be adopted best. Consider also that we will create another document for IPFIX which also defines the YANG message broker transformation.

chat.author: Mahesh Jethanandani
chat.text: On the policy-sharing draft, the only question I would have is if there is an overlap with other authorization/policy work.

chat.author: Mahesh Jethanandani
chat.text: @Thomas, what was the agreement from that discussion? Were IDR/GROW ok with the work to proceed in NMOP?

chat.author: Mahesh Jethanandani
chat.text: Or do you wan me to confirm with the RTG ADs.
